title: helianthemum vulgare var. tomentosum
intro: Helianthemum vulgare var. tomentosum, also known as the woolly rockrose, is a flowering plant species within the Cistaceae family, characterized by its pink to white flowers and dense, woolly foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum vulgare var. tomentosum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
